title: AB 309 to establish Social Housing in California passed off of the Assembly Floor with over a supermajority of votes and is now headed to the Senate. The final vote was 55-16.
prompt_preview:

Output JSON only matching the provided schema.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "In 2018, California ranked 49th among the United States in housing units per resident.",
    "An estimated 180,000 new units of housing need to be built each year in California to meet demand.",
    "Over 97% of cities and counties in California have been unable to produce enough affordable housing.",
    "There are only enough affordable and available rentals for 24% of extremely low income households in California.",
    "Publicly developed, maintained, and owned housing is the key to solving California's housing crisis.",
    "Social housing has been successful globally, including in Montgomery County, Maryland, Vienna, and Singapore.",
    "Social housing is protected from being sold to private for-profit entities for the duration of its life.",
    "Residents in social housing are granted the same protections as tenants in private properties, including just cause eviction protections.",
    "Housing for people with higher incomes will subsidize lower-income units, making developments self-sustaining and revenue neutral.",
    "Remaining funds from social housing developments will be used for community development and repairs.",
    "Social housing avoids concentrated poverty by creating mixed-income neighborhoods."
  ]
}
```
